reasoning: For 35+ countries to commit "as a matter of treaty obligation" to a highly specific set of five stringent prohibitions (including banning experiments, deployment, funding, and patent rights) by December 31, 2025, a treaty would realistically already need to be drafted, adopted, and open for signature. Domestic ratification of international treaties typically requires parliamentary approval and takes several years even after the treaty text is finalized. As of early 2025, while there is political momentum and academic support for a Solar Geoengineering Non-Use Agreement (e.g., calls by African nations at UNEA), there is no evidence that a formal binding treaty enforcing all five strict criteria has been adopted or opened for signature, let alone ratified by any country. Given the extremely tight 8.5-month timeline remaining, it is practically impossible for 35 countries to formally ratify such a treaty by the deadline. I am setting the probability to the minimum allowed bound of 2%.
